name: protocolsio-integration
tagline: Documents the protocols.io API v3 (search, create, publish protocols; manage steps, files, workspaces, discussions) via bearer-token REST ca
verdict: Clean, transparent API-integration skill: tokens are used only to call protocols.io directly, no exfiltration or hidden instructions found. Two real gaps for installers: irreversible actions (DOI publish, file/comment delete) have no built-in confirmation step, and repeated runs of create/publish workflows have no duplicate-avoidance guidance.

highlights.summary: Lists 'Delete and restore files' (line 99), comment deletion (line 69), and 'Publish protocols with DOI issuance' (line 56, and Workflow 2 step 6 at line 203) as normal operations with no instruction
No 'do not use when' / negative-trigger guidance anywhere in the file; a user can't tell from the skill when protocols.io integration is the wrong tool.

Create/publish workflows (Workflow 2, lines 194-205) give no guidance on checking for an existing protocol before creating, so re-running the workflow risks duplicate protocols or repeated DOI-publish
